steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The subject is 'The Proto-Atlantic-Congo language' (singular). The appositive phrase that follows renames it but does not change its number. The verb must agree with a singular subject.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — plural verb form: A plural verb does not agree with the singular subject 'The Proto-Atlantic-Congo language.' ✗
  • Choice B — 'is': 'Is' is the singular present-tense form that correctly agrees with the singular subject. ✓
  • Choice C — wrong auxiliary or form: This choice uses a form that does not satisfy singular subject-verb agreement. ✗
  • Choice D — wrong auxiliary or form: This choice also fails to match the singular subject. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
